title: Research
text:
We investigate how membrane-associated proteins regulate nuclear architecture, cellular stress responses, and transcriptional remodeling.



We study regulated intramembrane proteolysis and its role in activating membrane-bound transcription factors.



We combine structural modeling and molecular dynamics simulations to understand conformational changes and protein function.



We aim to connect molecular mechanisms with disease phenotypes and potential therapeutic strategies.

title: Welcome to Yong-Yeon Cho’s Lab
text:
We study the molecular mechanisms that regulate cellular architecture, membrane-associated signaling, and stress-responsive transcriptional programs.


Our laboratory focuses on the molecular basis of membrane biology, cellular stress responses, and disease-related mechanisms. By integrating cell biology, molecular biology, biochemical analysis, and computational approaches, we aim to uncover fundamental principles in health and disease.




Nuclear membrane biology
Stress-responsive transcription factors
Regulated intramembrane proteolysis
Structural and computational biology





Our lab website is under construction.
